trigger: Internal semantic setup audit after the count-level freeze and before runner binding found three design defects that would weaken the registered research questions.
defects: P1 test contained release/refuse only and all six task families shared one arithmetic implementation template.
P2 route targets combined arbitrary specialist labels with near-direct fallback, abstention, and clarification markers.
P3 deletion members were not explicitly declared initial-training members, making causal removal ambiguous.
changed_fields: P1 route allocation across development/calibration/test and family-specific release semantics
P2 overlapping allowed route features, specialist competence, and action-grounded request wording
P3 initial_training_member flag for train and deletion cohorts
the three affected corpus schema versions, identifiers, and content digests
unchanged_fields: P1 six families, nine effect surfaces, two seeds, calls, arms, endpoints, thresholds, and stop rules
P2 four families, six held-out actions with ten each, fifteen-case separate harm set, calls, arms, endpoints, thresholds, and stop rules
P3 six splits, five arms, three seeds, 24-state inventory, checkpoint authority, endpoints, thresholds, and stop rules
global budgets, support-state boundary, role separation, and non-claims
preservation: The exact superseded inputs and preregistration remain recoverable at commit 52925c426; their digests remain in that commit and no empirical result depended on them.
authorization: Allowed by the roadmap pre-outcome amendment rule; final preregistration remains blocked on code/setup binding.
non_claims: This amendment is design repair, not a favorable result.
Balanced forced coverage does not estimate natural prevalence.
No support state, residual, chapter, release, or format changes here.
